summary: Postinstall script exfiltrates filesystem secrets and AWS credentials to hardcoded remote IP
details: On npm install, postinstall.js automatically runs a payload that recursively walks /, /app, /home, /opt, and /root for files matching /flag|secret|env/, executes shell reconnaissance (id, uname, find, grep for HTB{} flags), and enumerates AWS credentials by invoking aws-cli to list and retrieve every accessible Secrets Manager secret (get-secret-value --query SecretString) and SSM parameter (get-parameter --with-decryption) across 6 regions, plus s3 ls. The collected output is chunked and PUT over plain HTTP to the hardcoded attacker endpoint http://154.57.164.80:30543. The script also includes explicit scanner-evasion: it short-circuits when the cwd/hostname/HOME matches /hscan|/home/scan|scan-|sandbox/, ensuring it runs against real installer hosts but not automated analysis sandboxes. Any host installing this package will leak filesystem secrets and any AWS credentials reachable via the default profile or instance role.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
